article_title: Sony Raises PS5 Prices Up to $150 Worldwide as Memory Chip Shortage and Tariffs Squeeze the Console Market

content_preview.title: Sony Raises PS5 Prices Up to $150 Worldwide as Memory Chip Shortage and Tariffs Squeeze the Console Market
content_preview.summary: Sony's second PlayStation 5 price increase in under a year took effect on April 2, pushing the PS5 Pro to $900 and the standard console to $650 as AI-driven memory demand and trade tariffs compress margins across the gaming hardware industry.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Sony Interactive Entertainment raised prices on every PlayStation 5 console and the PlayStation Portal remote player worldwide on April 2, 2026, marking the second global price increase for the PS5 lineup in under a year. The PS5 Pro received the steepest hike at $150, bringing it to $899.99 in the United States, while the standard PS5 and digital edition each rose by $100 to $649.99 and $599.99 respectively. The PlayStation Portal climbed $50 to $249.99.

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive coverage with detailed pricing breakdown, historical context, and supply chain analysis. Well-structured.
editor_notes.source_verification: PlayStation Blog (official): verified — all prices, quotes from Isabelle Tomatis confirmed. Fox Business: verified — memory chip shortage, Q4 2025 16% sales decline to 8M units, 'careful evaluation' quote confirmed. Kotaku: verified — lifetime price increases (30% standard, 50% digital), tariff impact, studio closures context confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All price points, percentages, and quotes verified against sources. Mathematical calculations (30% and 50% lifetime increases) are accurate.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Presents consumer frustration factually without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: No prior PS5 pricing coverage in the archive. Fresh topic.
